---
description: "Use when writing, reviewing, or restructuring project documentation, README files, specs, or user guides. Covers Markdown formatting, structure, and linking conventions."
---
# Documentation Guidelines

- Write in plain Markdown — no HTML unless necessary.
- Use ATX-style headings (`#`, `##`, `###`).
- Include a purpose statement at the top of every document.
- Link to other project docs rather than duplicating content.
- Use tables for structured data; use lists for sequential steps.
- Keep lines under 100 characters where practical (for diff readability).
- Add a "Quick Start" or "How to Use" section in user-facing docs.
